commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces Microsoft Foundry connection-test failure `response.text()` reads with an 8 KiB `readResponseTextLimited` reader and adds a focused Vitest regression.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +73. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `main` calls `res.text()` on failed Microsoft Foundry probe responses. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus ordinary maintainer review.

target.title: fix(microsoft-foundry): bound connection test error reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Microsoft Foundry onboarding connection tests could read unbounded HTTP error bodies via `response.text()` when the probe endpoint returns 400 or other failure statuses, risking memory pressure during provider setup.

## Why This Change Was Made

The connection test only displays the first 200 characters of error text to operators, but the previous implementation still buffered the entire response body. This switches both error branches to `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KiB cap, matching other provider HTTP error handling in the repo.

## User Impact

Operators running Foundry connection tests during onboarding still see the same warning notes for failed probes, without loading arbitrarily large error payloads into the gateway process.

## Evidence

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/microsoft-foundry/onboard.connection.test.ts
```

Added a regression test that returns an oversized error body through a tracked stream, asserts `response.text()` is never called, and verifies the stream is canceled after the bounded read.

AI-assisted: implementation and tests were drafted with Cursor agent assistance; validation was run locally with the command above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
